items.title: Outbreak of highly contagious infection grows to more than 220 cases

children.content: Federal and state governments have mobilised to address the largest outbreak of diphtheria in recent memory, with the Commonwealth preparing a support package for a disease once considered almost eradicated.

articles.title: Health officials warn cases of diphtheria could rise

children.content: Cases of diphtheria could continue to rise as the number of people affected reaches 220 across the country — the biggest outbreak of the disease since national records began in 1991.

image.altText: A nurse injecting a needle into the arm of a patient who is getting vaccinated.

articles.title: Diphtheria is spreading in Australia and so is misinformation about it

children.content: Falsehoods about diphtheria are spreading online as a worsening outbreak of the disease affects Indigenous communities.

image.altText: Falsehoods about diphtheria

items.title: Australia's costliest disaster recovery program fails to build single home

children.content: A new report finds a disaster recovery program set up after the devastating 2022 floods in northern NSW is yet to deliver any of the homes it promised.

image.altText: A woman looks out of her first story window as flood water laps at hair stair case, her whole yard covered in brown water.

items.title: South Sydney star retires from rugby league due to MND diagnosis

children.content: Rabbitohs and Maroons enforcer Jai Arrow announces he will step away from rugby league after being diagnosed with motor neurone disease.

image.altText: Jai Arrow at his retirement press conference

items.title: Labor walks tightrope as tax overhaul alarms startups and investors

children.content: Labor MPs are gearing up for a hard sell on the impact of the federal government's tax changes on some small businesses, trusts and shares, despite many reporting "positive" feedback on housing reforms.

children.content: Treasurer
Jim Chalmers has rejected NSW Premier Chris Minns's
comments on bracket creep. Follow live.

articles.title: AI Albo tax meme promoter ditched nuance to go viral

children.content: One startup founder who helped spread viral memes about capital gains tax reforms says the campaign was aimed at attracting attention after the budget, and not necessarily focused on accuracy.

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese says Australia stands with the family of a five-year-old Aboriginal girl, whose body was found on the outskirts of Alice Springs last month.

items.title: Death of Peter Hollingworth sparks difficult feelings for abuse victim

children.content: Former governor-general Peter Hollingworth, who resigned over his handling of child abuse in the church, died in Melbourne on Tuesday at the age of 91.

image.altText: An older man in a suit walking in front of a stone wall

items.title: Doctor fined for book of 'WTF' surgery pictures taken without consent

children.content: An anaesthetist who worked at Toowoomba Hospital has been fined $30,000 for professional misconduct after he included confidential patient information in a self-published book.

image.altText: A doctor in scrubs and with a headband is standing in front of a piece of medical equipment.

children.content: The two leaders are expected to discuss bilateral economic relations and "key international and regional issues", according to the Kremlin. Follow live.

items.title: Greenland tells US again it is 'not for sale' after Trump envoy talks

children.content: Jeff Landry, appointed by Donald Trump in December as the US special envoy to Greenland, has met the country's prime minister in the capital Nuuk.

image.altText: Lines of snow-covered houses seen on the edge of a snowy coastline, alongside dark ocean water.

items.title: Maldives investigates if Italian divers went too deep in fatal cave dive

children.content: A Maldives government official says a permit was given to study soft corals, but authorities were unaware the group planned to cave dive.

items.title: Married at First Sight UK allegations

children.content: A British network has entirely removed Married at First Sight UK from air and its streaming channel after allegations of rape and sexual assault.
The show is widely seen as controversial with accusations it normalises or even platforms toxic relationships.
Here's what we know about the allegations so far.

items.title: Australian government calls for detained flotilla members to be released

children.content: The Australian government says it is engaging with Israel over the welfare of 11 Australians detained, as Israeli forces fire "non-lethal" shots at two boats in a Gaza-bound aid flotilla.

items.title: Paedophile former magistrate to be released on home detention

children.content: Paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy will be released from jail when his sentence expires next month, despite an ongoing application to have him indefinitely detained.

items.title: One killed, four injured in 'brazen, violent attack' inside Sydney home

children.content: A man with links to rap group OneFour has died and four others have been seriously injured following a Canley Heights shooting, which is believed to be organised crime-related.

items.title: ASX falls below 8,500 level as rate rise fears surface

children.content: The Australian share market has fallen below 8,500 points after a US bond sell-off triggered further losses on Wall Street. Oil prices slipped after Donald Trump said he had been an hour away from ordering an attack on Iran before postponing it. Follow live.

image.altText: Shares on a screen at the ASX

items.title: Former NT child protection staff speak out after Kumanjayi Little Baby case

children.content: In the three weeks since the alleged murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby in Alice Springs, the NT's child protection system has come under intense scrutiny. Now, former child protection workers are speaking out about why they believe the system is broken.

items.title: Alcaraz injury woes deepen, former champion out of Wimbledon

children.content: Carlos Alcaraz will miss Wimbledon as he continues his rehab from a wrist injury that has already ruled him out of the French Open.

items.title: Luai and Tigers headline NRL's Las Vegas quartet for 2027 season-openers

items.title: Mosque shooting victims hailed as heroes and mourned by community

children.content: Three people killed by two shooters at a San Diego mosque were beloved pillars of the community and died while saving 140 children in the building at the time of the attack, authorities say.

image.altText: Images of three smiling men in separate black digital TV frames, alongside US flags.

items.title: Why is there no vaccine for this Ebola outbreak?

children.content: The latest Ebola outbreak is caused by a strain of virus discovered nearly two decades ago, yet there is no vaccine. So what does this mean for people affected? And can vaccines be fast-tracked?

image.altText: Thermometers on a table with a healthcare worker in the background.

items.title: Miners plan to retrofit WA nickel plants to cash in on high gold prices

children.content: Idle infrastructure from WA's once-booming nickel industry is set to be retrofitted to produce gold bars amid historic high prices for the precious metal.

items.title: Apprehension as UK mining company enters Australian coal

children.content: Anglo American has operated metallurgical coal mines in Queensland since the turn of the century. Now, a UK-based company has bought them all.

items.title: Core Lithium restarts Northern Territory mine as price rallies

children.content: Core Lithium has restarted mining at its project near Darwin as prices surge close to $4,200 a tonne.

image.altText: an aerial photo of a mining processing plant.

items.title: Infant death sparks call for use of church land to tackle homelessness

children.content: Church land is being touted as a potential solution to the homelessness crisis in a southern NSW city still reeling from the death of a newborn baby in a tent almost three weeks ago.

image.altText: Homeless setup near Wagga beach with backdrop of church

items.title: Tour company fallout deepens as more travellers speak out

items.title: AVO application against Mark Latham withdrawn by former partner
